title: Add Margin% + Cost/Billed columns to per-buyer breakdown; surface CPL-billed + margin on Meta tab
brief:
Two changes:

(A) Profitability tab — Per-buyer breakdown table currently has: Buyer | Leads | Billed | Cost | Revenue | Net | CPL. Add two columns: **Cost/Billed** ($ cost / count billed; show '—' if billed = 0) and **Margin %** (net / revenue * 100; tone green if >= 30, amber if 0–30, red if < 0). Reorder columns so the two new ones sit next to Net. Also: in the headline card grid, drop the two redundant 'Meta Cost' and 'Meta Revenue' cards (they duplicate Internal Cost + Internal Revenue) and replace with a single prominent 'Cost / Billed' headline card so it's the first thing the user sees alongside Margin %.

(B) Meta tab — Surface the same two metrics. Today the Meta tab shows ad spend, leads, CPM, CPC, CTR. Add **Cost per Billed Lead** (totalSpend / billed-count for the same date range) and **Margin** (internal billed revenue − ad spend, with margin% sub-label) as the two leftmost KPI cards.

Use the existing `applyPooledCpl` + `econ` shape; do not modify the math. For Meta, you may need to also query billed lead count for the range — reuse `fetchEnrichedLeadsForRange`.
outputContract:
Markdown response:

1. **Plan** (5–10 lines)
2. **Files** — fenced blocks per file. Required: updated `profitability/page.tsx`, updated `meta/page.tsx` (and/or MetaTables.tsx if KPI cards live there). Only include files you actually change.
   ```typescript
   // FILE: <repo-relative-path>
   <full file contents>
   ```
3. **Verification** — a 3-bullet list of what to spot-check visually.

Keep light theme + plain Tailwind. Don't break existing tone classes. Match the Card pattern already in profitability/page.tsx.
